title: Bug? `arbtrace_transaction` returns `no trace found` when transaction exists
author: jwelch-qn
body:
Hi team, 

I got a transaction: https://arbiscan.io/tx/0x39367663b264e2690e32b8875166105b7f25fffe761c8ababded846bb50cd3d6

which is returned fine in `eth_getBlockByNumber`:
```
'{"method":"eth_getBlockByNumber","params":["0xeb1dbf",false],"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0"}'
```

It is not however return in `arbtrace_block` and not found in `arbtrace_transaction`
```{"method":"arbtrace_block","params":["0xeb1dbf"],"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0"}```
```'{"method":"arbtrace_transaction","params":["0x39367663b264e2690e32b8875166105b7f25fffe761c8ababded846bb50cd3d6"],"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0"}'```

This is with `offchainlabs/arb-node:v1.4.5-e97c1a4`

Checking blocks near this one and similar transactions I find they return okay.

title: Arbitrum on Mirror
author: YungSB
body: Would it be possible for Arbitrum to start using mirror.xyz to put out blogs posts? Mirror is a platform where people can subscriber to writers, mint / collect the blog posts, can mint NFTs in the blog posts, etc. Polygon, Scroll, Linea, Taiko, and so many others are using mirror for the exposure it brings to people outside of their ecosystem.

title: Extra datadir layer manipulating snapshot tar giving "no --init.* mode supplied and chain data not in expected directory" error

body:
https://developer.arbitrum.io/node-running/running-a-node#required-artifacts

2) couldn't init without "--init.*" flag

3) examining with 
```
--init.url="file:///XXXXXX.tar" --init.empty --l1.url=http://XXXXXX:8545 --l2.chain-id=42161 --http.api=net,web3,eth --http.corsdomain=* --http.addr=0.0.0.0 --http.vhosts=* --node.rpc.evm-timeout=0
```

against both

title: Eth_call Non Responsive For Block Range | Arbitrum Classic Legacy Client
author: billqn
body:
Arbitrum Classic: arb-rpc-node/v0.8.0

OS & Version: linux-amd64

`Eth_call` not responding for 'balanceOf' on Arbitrum Legacy client for block range `6835300-6835333`.  Call seems to just hang. 

Curl command to reproduce:
```
curl -s -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"method":"eth_call","params":[{"from":null,"to":"0xFd086bC7CD5C481DCC9C85ebE478A1C0b69FCbb9","data":"0x70a08231000000000000000000000000960ea3e3C7FB317332d990873d354E18d7645590"}, "0x684c64"],"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0"}' localhost:8547
```

Looking at logs in real-time while sending the cURL shows no error messages or exiting logs.

title: Metrics unavailable
author: jiOnederfull
body:
I restarted the classic node after it fully synced. And suddenly I couldn't check the values from the metrics.

It seems that its metrics server is not starting according to the logs.

## Metrics available
- It's still syncing.
- Never stopped before.
- Metrics works well.

```sh
Previous checkpoint,  total gas used: 0, L1 block: 0, L2 block: 0, log count: 0, messages count: 0, timestamp: Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
Reloading chain to the last checkpoint saved
Loading initial machine
{"level":"info","component":"monitor","time":"2023-03-28T08:25:42Z","caller":"/home/user/arb-node-core/monitor/monitor.go:83","message":"storage initialized"}
{"addr":"http://0.0.0.0:1070/debug/metrics","fn":"github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/metrics/exp.Setup","lvl":"info","msg":"Starting metrics server","t":"2023-03-28T08:25:42.885075164Z"}
{"level":"warn","component":"arb-node","time":"2023-03-28T08:25:42Z","caller":"/home/user/arb-rpc-node/cmd/arb-node/arb-node.go:292","message":"Missing --feed.input.url so not subscribing to feed"}
```

## Metrics unavailable
- Restarted after synced.
- Metrics doesn't work.
```sh

Loading initial machine

Last  valid database checkpoint,  total gas used: 94830154880212, L1 block: 15447143, L2 block: 22207741, log count: 47264171, messages count: 46238208, timestamp: Wed Aug 31 14:29:19 2022
```

title: Something wrong with eth_trace and amount of gas used
author: unordered-set
body:
This is a transaction of interest: https://arbiscan.io/tx/0x640d0052b19bbc5c1c478e7f33ecb3e15c0152453ea604efd8b99f78537583d2

```
$ cast t --rpc-url https://endpoints.omniatech.io/v1/arbitrum/one/public 0x640d0052b19bbc5c1c478e7f33ecb3e15c0152453ea604efd8b99f78537583d2

...
gas                  450000
...
```

Same in explorer. But transaction failed for no reason.

But when you show it in trace: https://arbiscan.io/vmtrace?txhash=0x640d0052b19bbc5c1c478e7f33ecb3e15c0152453ea604efd8b99f78537583d2 - gas countdown starts with 24433. trace_2 also shows same

title: Timestamp manipulation
author: stackmoon39
body:
Timestamp manipulation: There's a line with a warning about relying on time: block.timestamp.
 Be aware that block.timestamp can be manipulated by miners within a certain range

title: json: unsupported type: func() common.Hash
author: ko0f
body:
I'm running a node on Ubuntu 20.04.5 LTS using docker -
```
docker run --rm -it  -v /arbitrum:/home/user/.arbitrum -p 8547:8547 -p 8548:8548 offchainlabs/nitro-node:v2.0.10-73224e3 --l1.url=http://10.x.x.x:8545 --l2.chain-id=42161 --http.api=net,web3,eth,debug,txpool
```
it runs fine and when I attempt to query for sync status -
```
curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" --data '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"eth_syncing","params":[],"id":67}' http://localhost:8547
```
it replies -
```
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":67,"error":{"code":-32000,"message":"json: unsupported type: func() common.Hash"}}
```

In the past (a month or two) it worked, any idea what's the problem?
